manifest.title: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only t
manifest.caption: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only training producing Baseline I; (2) staged QAT with fake quantization starting at training_frac=0.5 leading to Baseline E (QAT+teacher); (3) codec-guided distillation variant F adding an explicit zlib-based compression loss L_comp on selected tensors; (4) optional compression-biased gating variant H with training-time stochastic gates driven by per-channel zlib statistics. The diagram should also depict the int8+zlib deployment roundtrip, the 16MB artifact cap, and the quick-gate runtime constraint (runtime <= 1.10x baseline).
manifest.label: fig:pipeline_overview_1

manifest.title: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillatio
manifest.caption: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillation (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network and an int8-student undergoing staged QAT. Indicate: (a) the standard distillation path with KL loss between teacher and quantized-student logits; (b) the selection of specific weight tensors or activations from the quantized student, conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, and computation of bits-per-byte; (c) the teacher- or baseline-relative target alpha and the resulting L_comp = (L_zlib - alpha)^2 term; (d) combination into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp. Make it clear that zlib is used sparsely during training and that deployment uses int8+zlib without extra components.
manifest.label: fig:architecture_diagram_2

manifest.title: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2)
manifest.caption: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) During selected minibatches, quantize per-channel activations, run zlib on each channel slice to obtain bpb_c, and update EMA statistics h_c; (2) compute biased drop probabilities p_drop(c) = clip(beta * (h_c - mean_h), 0, p_max); (3) sample binary gate g_c ~ Bernoulli(1 - p_drop(c)) and apply to activations during QAT; (4) emphasize that at inference, all g_c are fixed to 1 (no gating), so the deployed graph is unchanged. Include annotations about how this ties into the overall QAT stage and preserves the gate/runtime budget.
manifest.label: fig:method_flowchart_3

manifest.title: Runtime Overhead and Quick-Gate Constraint
manifest.caption: Runtime comparison between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aligned candidate, plotted in milliseconds with annotations for the runtime ratio and whether the quick-gate constraint (runtime ≤ 1.10× baseline) is satisfied.
manifest.label: fig:fig_runtime_quick_gate

manifest.title: Validation bpb Comparison: Float Training vs QAT + Roundtrip Alignment
manifest.caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aware compression alignment configuration, both under the same gate budget and artifact size limit. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance.
manifest.label: fig:fig_main_results_val_bpb

manifest.title: Change in Validation bpb Relative to Baseline
manifest.caption: Absolute change in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) of the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration relative to the float-only baseline, highlighting the net compression benefit after the int8+zlib roundtrip.
manifest.label: fig:fig_delta_bpb_vs_baseline

manifest.title: Feasibility Under Runtime and Artifact Constraints
manifest.caption: Summary of whether the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration satisfies the quick-gate runtime constraint and the 16 MB artifact size limit, providing an at-a-glance feasibility check for deployment relative to the baseline.
manifest.label: fig:fig_gate_feasibility_overview

![Figure 1: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only training producing Baseline I; (2) staged QAT with fake quantization starting at training_frac=0.5 leading to Baseline E (QAT+teacher); (3) codec-guided distillation variant F adding an explicit zlib-based compression loss L_comp on selected tensors; (4) optional compression-biased gating variant H with training-time stochastic gates driven by per-channel zlib statistics. The diagram should also depict the int8+zlib deployment roundtrip, the 16MB artifact cap, and the quick-gate runtime constraint (runtime <= 1.10x baseline).](charts/pipeline_overview_1.png)

![Figure 2: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillation (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network and an int8-student undergoing staged QAT. Indicate: (a) the standard distillation path with KL loss between teacher and quantized-student logits; (b) the selection of specific weight tensors or activations from the quantized student, conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, and computation of bits-per-byte; (c) the teacher- or baseline-relative target alpha and the resulting L_comp = (L_zlib - alpha)^2 term; (d) combination into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp. Make it clear that zlib is used sparsely during training and that deployment uses int8+zlib without extra components.](charts/architecture_diagram_2.png)

![Figure 3: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) During selected minibatches, quantize per-channel activations, run zlib on each channel slice to obtain bpb_c, and update EMA statistics h_c; (2) compute biased drop probabilities p_drop(c) = clip(beta * (h_c - mean_h), 0, p_max); (3) sample binary gate g_c ~ Bernoulli(1 - p_drop(c)) and apply to activations during QAT; (4) emphasize that at inference, all g_c are fixed to 1 (no gating), so the deployed graph is unchanged. Include annotations about how this ties into the overall QAT stage and preserves the gate/runtime budget.](charts/method_flowchart_3.png)

![Figure 4: Runtime comparison between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aligned candidate, plotted in milliseconds with annotations for the runtime ratio and whether the quick-gate constraint (runtime ≤ 1.10× baseline) is satisfied.](charts/fig_runtime_quick_gate.png)

![Figure 5: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aware compression alignment configuration, both under the same gate budget and artifact size limit. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance.](charts/fig_main_results_val_bpb.png)

![Figure 6: Absolute change in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) of the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration relative to the float-only baseline, highlighting the net compression benefit after the int8+zlib roundtrip.](charts/fig_delta_bpb_vs_baseline.png)

![Figure 7: Summary of whether the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration satisfies the quick-gate runtime constraint and the 16 MB artifact size limit, providing an at-a-glance feasibility check for deployment relative to the baseline.](charts/fig_gate_feasibility_overview.png)

## AVAILABLE FIGURES (embed in the paper)
The following figures were generated from actual experiment data. Reference them in the appropriate paper sections using markdown image syntax: `![Caption](charts/filename.png)`

**Figure 1** (`charts/pipeline_overview_1.png`) — High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only t
  Caption: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only training producing Baseline I; (2) staged QAT with fake quantization starting at training_frac=0.5 leading to Baseline E (QAT+teacher); (3) codec-guided distillation variant F adding an explicit zlib-based compression loss L_comp on selected tensors; (4) optional compression-biased gating variant H with training-time stochastic gates driven by per-channel zlib statistics. The diagram should also depict the int8+zlib deployment roundtrip, the 16MB artifact cap, and the quick-gate runtime constraint (runtime <= 1.10x baseline).
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 2** (`charts/architecture_diagram_2.png`) — Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillatio
  Caption: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillation (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network and an int8-student undergoing staged QAT. Indicate: (a) the standard distillation path with KL loss between teacher and quantized-student logits; (b) the selection of specific weight tensors or activations from the quantized student, conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, and computation of bits-per-byte; (c) the teacher- or baseline-relative target alpha and the resulting L_comp = (L_zlib - alpha)^2 term; (d) combination into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp. Make it clear that zlib is used sparsely during training and that deployment uses int8+zlib without extra components.
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 3** (`charts/method_flowchart_3.png`) — Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2)
  Caption: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) During selected minibatches, quantize per-channel activations, run zlib on each channel slice to obtain bpb_c, and update EMA statistics h_c; (2) compute biased drop probabilities p_drop(c) = clip(beta * (h_c - mean_h), 0, p_max); (3) sample binary gate g_c ~ Bernoulli(1 - p_drop(c)) and apply to activations during QAT; (4) emphasize that at inference, all g_c are fixed to 1 (no gating), so the deployed graph is unchanged. Include annotations about how this ties into the overall QAT stage and preserves the gate/runtime budget.
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 4** (`charts/fig_runtime_quick_gate.png`) — Runtime Overhead and Quick-Gate Constraint
  Caption: Runtime comparison between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aligned candidate, plotted in milliseconds with annotations for the runtime ratio and whether the quick-gate constraint (runtime ≤ 1.10× baseline) is satisfied.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 5** (`charts/fig_main_results_val_bpb.png`) — Validation bpb Comparison: Float Training vs QAT + Roundtrip Alignment
  Caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aware compression alignment configuration, both under the same gate budget and artifact size limit. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 6** (`charts/fig_delta_bpb_vs_baseline.png`) — Change in Validation bpb Relative to Baseline
  Caption: Absolute change in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) of the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration relative to the float-only baseline, highlighting the net compression benefit after the int8+zlib roundtrip.
  Place in: **Analysis** section

**Figure 7** (`charts/fig_gate_feasibility_overview.png`) — Feasibility Under Runtime and Artifact Constraints
  Caption: Summary of whether the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration satisfies the quick-gate runtime constraint and the 16 MB artifact size limit, providing an at-a-glance feasibility check for deployment relative to the baseline.
  Place in: **Analysis** section
